summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on three zoning cases. The Woodhall Crossing subdivision final development plan (ZC-2023-007) was tabled due to unresolved issues including incomplete preliminary requirements, fence placement discrepancies, traffic signal timing, and the developer's absence. The Lakefield Place PUD expansion (ZC-2024-006) was approved as a new PUD with conditions regarding a second entrance per Fire Department requirements. The Health Source Solutions major modification (ZC-2024-007) was approved with staff-recommended conditions for concrete removal, additional landscaping, and uniform lighting.
decisions: Motion to table Zoning Case 2023-007 (Woodhall Crossing final development plan Section 1) passed unanimously to allow clarification on outstanding issues
Motion to approve Zoning Case 2024-006 (Lakefield Place PUD expansion) as a new PUD with condition that second entrance/emergency access be addressed per Fire Department concerns passed unanimously
Motion to approve Zoning Case 2024-007 (Health Source Solutions major modification) with staff conditions (removal of concrete entrance, additional landscaping, uniform lighting) passed unanimously
December 3rd meeting canceled due to lack of agenda items
Approval of October 1, 2024 video meeting minutes passed unanimously
action_items: Max Merritt to share chairperson's questions with Woodhall Crossing applicant/developer
Woodhall Crossing developer to clarify fence placement along Cornwell properties to match Board of Trustees preliminary approval conditions
Woodhall Crossing developer to address tree survey requirement from preliminary approval
Woodhall Crossing developer to clarify timeline for traffic signal installation and vertical curb improvements on State Route 48
Woodhall Crossing developer to provide floor plans meeting Section 6.02 C3C requirements
Lakefield Place developer to address secondary emergency access road requirement from Fire Department
Lakefield Place developer to complete traffic impact study required by Clermont County Engineer's Office
Health Source Solutions to remove existing concrete entrance and pavement from State Route 28 frontage
Health Source Solutions to add landscaping to screen parking area from State Route 28 per corridor plan
Health Source Solutions to discuss cross-access easement with Clermont County engineers
January meeting to be scheduled after Board of Trustees completes reorganization and 2025 schedule
key_amounts: Woodhall Crossing Section 1: 54 lots proposed out of 200+ total in preliminary plan
Woodhall Crossing: 2.86 units per acre density
Woodhall Crossing: 5 acres of open space, 27% of site
Woodhall Crossing: 180-foot vinyl privacy fence proposed along Cornwell properties
Woodhall Crossing: Traffic signal required before 132nd home is occupied per ODOT
Woodhall Crossing: 43 of 54 lots less than 8,000 square feet
Lakefield Place expansion: 107 lots proposed on 40+ acres
Lakefield Place: 2.62 units per acre density
Lakefield Place: 13 acres (32%) open space, slightly below 35% required
Health Source Solutions: Building expansion from 7,700 sq ft to 16,234 sq ft
Health Source Solutions: 98 parking spaces proposed, 81 required
Health Source Solutions: Approximately 10,000 sq ft of existing leftover pavement to be removed
$75 late fee on $25 monthly HOA payment mentioned by resident regarding existing Coach Development community
notable_topics: Woodhall Crossing final development plan Section 1 - tabled due to multiple unresolved issues
Developer absence and accountability concerns for Woodhall Crossing (Coach Development/Robert Gerwin)
Traffic signal timing and vertical curb improvements on State Route 48 for Woodhall Crossing
Privacy fence and screening requirements along Cornwell properties not matching preliminary approval
Construction activity occurring before final development plan approval concerns
Resident complaints about developer communication and HOA management in existing Coach Development subdivision
Lakefield Place Phase 2 PUD expansion with 107 additional lots
Fire Department requiring secondary emergency access road for Lakefield Place
Health Source Solutions building expansion funded by Appalachia Community Grant Program
State Route 28 corridor plan compliance and landscaping screening
Tree survey requirement from preliminary approval not fulfilled
Future concerns about buffering between residential and manufacturing properties (Phoenix Drive)
attendees: Max Merritt (Zoning Consultant for Goshen Township)
Edwin Perugia (Lakefield Place developer, 7861 East Kemper Road)
James Brostrick (Director of Facilities, Health Source of Ohio)
notes: Significant tension regarding Woodhall Crossing developer Robert Gerwin (Coach Development) who was absent from the meeting. Residents from existing subdivision reported ongoing issues with developer communication, HOA document confusion, unreasonable fees, and lack of financial transparency. The Zoning Commission chair expressed frustration that preliminary approval conditions were not being met before seeking final approval, including construction occurring on site before final plan approval in potential violation of zoning resolution. A homeowners meeting with Paul Coors (Ryan Homes development manager for Cincinnati) was scheduled for the next day at 5:30 PM. Mr. Cornwell emotionally noted his family has been on State Route 48 for almost 100 years and is now moving due to the development. The Lakefield Place case was initially submitted as a major modification but was reclassified as a new PUD per county prosecutor guidance to avoid referendum issues. Mr. Frisbee was called during roll but did not respond - appears absent. December meeting canceled; next meeting likely February 2025 pending Board of Trustees reorganization.
title: ZC 11-12-24
